summary.intent: Fix a failing CI job in the moltis repository caused by an invalid apt package name for GLSL shader compilation tools.
summary.outcome: Replaced the non-existent `glslc` apt package with the correct `glslang-tools` package in CI workflows and release config, then committed and pushed the fix.

repo: The project uses LunarG Vulkan SDK apt repo for Ubuntu 22.04 (jammy) to install build dependencies for Vulkan/GPU support.
llama-cpp-2 with the 'vulkan' feature requires GLSL shader compilation tools as a build dependency.
Commit 681e9f5b introduced the regression by replacing valid 'glslang-tools' with non-existent 'glslc' package.
glslc is not a standalone apt package in the LunarG jammy repo — glslang-tools is the correct package name.

code.finding: Two occurrences of glslc needed fixing: one in the Rust CI job and one in the scheduled full targets job.
One occurrence of glslc in release workflow also needed fixing.
Documentation referenced glslc and was updated to stay consistent with the workflow fix.

workflow: LunarG Vulkan SDK package list for jammy can be verified at https://packages.lunarg.com/vulkan/dists/jammy/main/binary-amd64/Packages.
GitHub Actions API can be used to fetch job logs and check run status before a run completes.

friction: A security hook flagged the workflow file edits as informational warnings, requiring edits to be retried.
The LunarG repo does not have a 'glslc' or 'shaderc' package, which required fetching the package list to confirm the correct name.
open_items: No Rust source code directly references glslc/glslang/shaderc — the dependency is transitive through llama-cpp-2's vulkan feature; worth documenting this dependency chain explicitly.
